what_it_does: This file is a metadata record that attaches a channel-memory item to a board item, declaring the attachment type, target board item URI, and creation timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
No body content beyond the frontmatter
standouts: The target is referenced via a board:// URI scheme
The file is purely a linking/attachment record with no explanatory content
The attachment type board_items signals a channel-to-board relationship
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Follows a ledger-style attachment naming scheme att-<id>.md
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset
concerns: Contains no context about what is being attached or why
The referenced board item is not described within this file
No author or source information is recorded
notes: A minimal channel-to-board attachment ledger entry that links dragnet-development content to board item addd2919.
questions: What content or message in dragnet-development is being attached?
What does board item addd2919 represent?
Who created this attachment and for what reason?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be an attachment metadata record and it functions exactly as that.
intention_signals: It is trying to persist the fact that something in the dragnet-development channel is attached to a specific board item, preserving the relationship and timestamp.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI scheme to reference a target board item is a distinct linking convention.
contribution: It contributes a cross-reference record that preserves the existence, type, target, and timing of a channel-to-board attachment, which a fused system could use to reconstruct relationship provenance.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record linking a dragnet-development channel item to board://item-addd2919 with a timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to record that a channel-memory attachment of type board_items points to a specific board item.
